vocabulary.definition: The total area of all the faces (outer surfaces) of a three-dimensional figure
vocabulary.visual: A box 6 × 4 × 2: SA = 2(24) + 2(12) + 2(8) = 88 in² — like wrapping paper needed

vocabulary.definition: A two-dimensional pattern that can be folded to form a three-dimensional figure
vocabulary.visual: Cut along the edges of a cereal box and unfold it flat — you see all 6 faces

vocabulary.definition: A flat surface of a three-dimensional figure
vocabulary.visual: A rectangular prism has 6 faces: top, bottom, front, back, left, right

vocabulary.definition: A flat shape that has only length and width (no height)
vocabulary.visual: A rectangle drawn on paper is 2D — it has length and width but no depth

vocabulary.definition: A line segment where two faces of a solid meet
vocabulary.visual: A cube has 12 edges — the lines where two flat faces connect

launch.narrative: Your class is designing custom wrapping paper for time capsule boxes. To figure out how much paper you need, you have to find the total surface area of each box. The trick? Unfold the box into a net so you can see every face laid flat!
launch.contextImage: Students unfolding a rectangular box into a flat net pattern to measure wrapping paper
noticePrompts: How many faces does a rectangular prism have?
What shapes do you see when the box is unfolded?
Are all the faces the same size?
wonderPrompts: Is there more than one way to unfold a box into a net?
How does a net help you find surface area more easily than looking at the 3D shape?

explore.instructions: Identify each face of the rectangular time capsule box (l = 8 in, w = 5 in, h = 3 in). Find the area of each face pair, then calculate the total surface area.

discourse.prompt: The total surface area is 158 in². Which face pair contributes the most area? Why does that make sense when you look at the dimensions?
discourse.sentenceFrame: The ___ faces contribute the most area (___ in²) because their dimensions ___ × ___ = ___ are the largest. The total SA is ___ + ___ + ___ = ___ in².

optional.stem: A net of a cube has how many square faces?
optional.explanation: A cube has 6 faces, so its net has 6 squares.

optional.stem: A cube has edges of 3 cm. What is its surface area?
Why does a net help find surface area?
choices: It shows every face flat so you can add up their areas
optional.explanation: A net unfolds the solid so all faces are visible to measure and add.

optional.stem: A rectangular box net shows faces of 6 cm², 6 cm², 8 cm², 8 cm², 12 cm², 12 cm². What is the surface area?

approaching.stem: A rectangular prism has l = 6 in, w = 4 in, h = 2 in. What is its surface area?
How many faces does a rectangular prism have?
approaching.explanation: A rectangular prism has 6 faces: top, bottom, front, back, left, and right.

approaching.stem: Surface area is measured in which type of unit?
approaching.explanation: Surface area measures flat surfaces, so it uses square units. Volume uses cubic units.

approaching.label: Match each concept about rectangular prisms.
Put these steps in order for finding the surface area of a 5 × 3 × 2 box.

onLevel.instructions: Find the surface area of each time capsule box using its net.

onLevel.stem: A cube has edges of 5 cm. What is its surface area?
onLevel.explanation: A cube has 6 identical faces. Each face: 5 × 5 = 25 cm². Total SA = 6 × 25 = 150 cm².

onLevel.label: A gift box has SA = 222 in². The top and bottom together are 120 in². The front and back together are 60 in². How much area is the left and right sides together?
onLevel.questionText: What is the combined area of the left and right faces?

onLevel.label: Sort these rectangular prisms from LEAST surface area to GREATEST surface area.

extending.title: Find the Surface Area Error

workedExample.label: Dimensions
workedExample.work: l = 10 in, w = 4 in, h = 3 in

extending.correctWork: The student forgot to multiply the last face pair by 2. It should be 2(4×3) = 24, not 12. Correct: SA = 80 + 60 + 24 = 164 in².
hints: Check Step 3: how many times does each face pair appear?
Each face pair has 2 matching faces. The student only counted one left/right face.

extending.prompt: Two boxes have the same volume of 60 in³. Box A is 5 × 4 × 3. Box B is 10 × 6 × 1. Which box uses less wrapping paper (has less surface area)? Show your calculations and explain why a more cube-like shape might use less material.
extending.sentenceFrame: Box A: SA = 2(___) + 2(___) + 2(___) = ___ in². Box B: SA = 2(___) + 2(___) + 2(___) = ___ in². Box ___ uses less wrapping paper because ___.

extending.instructions: Compare volume and surface area. Which measurement is bigger for each prism?

connect.scenario: A gift shop wraps boxes with decorative paper. A box is 14 inches long, 10 inches wide, and 6 inches tall. One sheet of wrapping paper covers 400 square inches.
connect.promptQuestion: How many sheets of wrapping paper are needed to cover the entire box?
connect.prompt: The box's surface area is ___ in² because SA = 2(___×___) + 2(___×___) + 2(___×___) = ___ + ___ + ___ = ___. You need ___ sheets because ___ ÷ 400 ≈ ___.

exitTicket.stem: A rectangular prism has l = 9 in, w = 5 in, h = 3 in. What is the surface area?
exitTicket.explanation: SA = 2(9×5) + 2(9×3) + 2(5×3) = 90 + 54 + 30 = 174 in². Surface area uses square units (in²).
